project: UP Platform Phase 1: Plugin System & Hook Pipeline
description: Add a declarative plugin system with auto-discovery, polyglot hook pipeline, and Markdown rules engine to up-cli. This transforms up-cli from a closed Python tool into an extensible platform.

userStories.title: Plugin manifest schema and directory structure
userStories.description: Define the plugin.json schema and standard directory layout. Create the PluginManifest dataclass and validation logic. Plugins live in .up/plugins/ with auto-discovery by convention.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/up/plugins/__init__.py and src/up/plugins/manifest.py
PluginManifest dataclass with fields: name, version, description, author, engines (list), category (enum: safety|productivity|quality|learning)
Validation: name must be kebab-case, version must be semver, engines defaults to ['all']
from_json(path) class method that reads and validates plugin.json
Unit tests in tests/test_plugin_manifest.py
userStories.notes: Foundation for everything else. Keep it minimal — just the data model.

userStories.title: Plugin loader with auto-discovery
userStories.description: Create PluginLoader that scans .up/plugins/installed/ and .up/plugins/builtin/ directories, reads plugin.json from each, and builds a registry of available plugins with their components (commands/, hooks/, rules/, agents/, skills/).
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/up/plugins/loader.py with PluginLoader class
discover_plugins(workspace: Path) -> List[LoadedPlugin] scans both installed/ and builtin/ dirs
LoadedPlugin dataclass holds: manifest, path, components dict (commands=[], hooks=[], rules=[], agents=[], skills=[])
Components auto-discovered by scanning subdirectories: *.md in commands/, hooks.json in hooks/, *.md in rules/
Graceful handling: skip plugins with invalid manifests, log warnings
Unit tests with temp directory fixtures in tests/test_plugin_loader.py
userStories.notes: This is the core discovery mechanism. No execution yet — just finding and cataloging plugins.

userStories.title: Plugin registry with enable/disable state
userStories.description: Create PluginRegistry that persists plugin state (enabled/disabled) in .up/plugins/registry.json. Provides lookup methods for active plugins and their components.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/up/plugins/registry.py with PluginRegistry class
registry.json schema: {plugins: [{name, version, enabled, installed_at, source}]}
Methods: get_enabled() -> List[LoadedPlugin], is_enabled(name) -> bool, enable(name), disable(name)
load() reads registry.json + runs PluginLoader.discover_plugins(), merges state
save() writes registry.json atomically (temp file + os.replace, same pattern as StateManager)
New plugins found on disk are auto-registered as enabled
Unit tests in tests/test_plugin_registry.py
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 and US-002. Uses same atomic write pattern as core/state.py.

userStories.title: CLI commands: up plugin list|enable|disable
userStories.description: Add 'up plugin' command group with list, enable, and disable subcommands. Uses Rich tables for display.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/up/commands/plugin.py with Click command group
Register in cli.py: main.add_command(plugin_group)
'up plugin list' shows table: Name | Version | Status | Category | Components
'up plugin enable <name>' enables a disabled plugin
'up plugin disable <name>' disables an enabled plugin
Rich formatting: enabled=green, disabled=dim, component counts shown
Error handling: unknown plugin name shows helpful message
userStories.notes: Depends on US-003. Simple CLI wrapper around PluginRegistry.

userStories.title: Plugin scaffold command: up plugin create
userStories.description: Add 'up plugin create <name>' that scaffolds a new plugin directory with plugin.json, empty component directories, and a README.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'create' subcommand to plugin group in src/up/commands/plugin.py
'up plugin create my-plugin' creates .up/plugins/installed/my-plugin/ with: plugin.json, commands/, hooks/, rules/, README.md
plugin.json pre-filled with name, version 0.1.0, empty description
README.md has basic structure explaining each directory
Auto-registers in registry as enabled
Error if plugin name already exists
userStories.notes: Depends on US-003. Lowers barrier to creating plugins.

userStories.title: Hook pipeline runner with JSON I/O
userStories.description: Create HookRunner that executes hook scripts (any language) via subprocess with JSON stdin and exit code semantics. This is the polyglot execution layer.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/up/plugins/hooks.py with HookRunner class
HookSpec dataclass: type (command|prompt), command (str), timeout (int, default 10), matcher (optional regex)
run_hook(spec, event_data: dict) -> HookResult where HookResult has: allowed (bool), message (str), exit_code (int)
Passes event_data as JSON via stdin to subprocess
Exit code semantics: 0=allow, 1=warn (log stderr, continue), 2=block (log stderr, halt)
Timeout enforcement via subprocess timeout parameter
Handles missing commands, permission errors, and crashes gracefully
Unit tests with simple bash/python test hooks in tests/test_hook_runner.py
userStories.notes: Core execution engine for hooks. Language-agnostic via JSON stdin/stdout.

userStories.title: Hook pipeline integration with EventBridge
userStories.description: Wire HookRunner into the existing EventBridge so that plugin hooks are automatically invoked when matching events fire. Add new EventTypes for hook lifecycle.
acceptanceCriteria: Add EventTypes to events.py: PRE_TOOL_USE, POST_TOOL_USE, PRE_EXECUTE, POST_EXECUTE
Create src/up/plugins/bridge.py with PluginEventBridge that connects PluginRegistry hooks to EventBridge
On EventBridge.emit(), matching plugin hooks are executed via HookRunner
Hook matcher field filters by event data (e.g., tool_name regex match)
If any hook returns exit_code=2, the event is marked as blocked
Hook results are logged to event_log for observability
Integration test: register a test hook, emit event, verify hook was called
userStories.notes: Depends on US-003 and US-006. This is the key integration point between the plugin system and up-cli's existing event architecture.

userStories.title: Markdown rules engine
userStories.description: Create RulesEngine that parses Markdown rule files (YAML frontmatter + body) and converts them into hook-like event handlers. Rules are the simplest extension point — no code required.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/up/plugins/rules.py with RulesEngine class
RuleSpec dataclass: name, event (str), pattern (regex), action (block|warn|allow), confidence (int 0-100), message (str from markdown body)
parse_rule(path: Path) -> RuleSpec reads .md file, extracts YAML frontmatter and body
evaluate(rule: RuleSpec, event_data: dict) -> HookResult applies regex pattern to event data, returns result based on action
RulesEngine.load_rules(plugin_path) discovers all .md files in rules/ directory
Integration with PluginEventBridge: rules are treated as lightweight hooks
Unit tests with sample rule files in tests/test_rules_engine.py
userStories.notes: Depends on US-006. Uses same HookResult type. YAML frontmatter parsing via PyYAML (already a dependency).

userStories.title: Builtin safety plugin: checkpoint and circuit breaker hooks
userStories.description: Refactor existing checkpoint and circuit breaker logic into a builtin plugin that ships with UP. This proves the plugin system works with real functionality.
acceptanceCriteria: Create .up/plugins/builtin/safety/ with plugin.json and hooks/
Pre-execute hook: auto-creates checkpoint before AI operations (wraps existing checkpoint.py logic)
Post-execute hook: checks circuit breaker state, blocks if OPEN (wraps existing state.py logic)
Hook implementations in Python, invoked via HookRunner
Existing up save/reset/start commands still work (they call the same underlying logic)
Integration test: emit PRE_EXECUTE event, verify checkpoint created
userStories.notes: Depends on US-007. This is the proof-of-concept: existing up-cli safety features running as plugins.

userStories.title: Builtin verify plugin: test/lint/typecheck hooks
userStories.description: Refactor existing verification logic into a builtin plugin with confidence-based scoring instead of binary pass/fail.
acceptanceCriteria: Create .up/plugins/builtin/verify/ with plugin.json and hooks/
Post-tool-use hook: runs configured test/lint/typecheck commands after file modifications
VerifyResult includes confidence score (0-100) based on: all pass=100, lint warnings=80, test failures=20, crash=0
Configurable threshold in .up/config.json: verify.min_confidence (default 70)
Below threshold: blocks commit. Above threshold: allows with warnings logged.
Hook reads verification commands from .up/config.json (test_cmd, lint_cmd, typecheck_cmd)
Unit tests in tests/test_verify_plugin.py
userStories.notes: Depends on US-007. Upgrades binary pass/fail to confidence scoring (from claude-code's code-reviewer pattern).

userStories.title: Plugin install command: up plugin install <path>
userStories.description: Add 'up plugin install' that copies a plugin from a local path into .up/plugins/installed/ and registers it.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'install' subcommand to plugin group
'up plugin install /path/to/my-plugin' copies directory to .up/plugins/installed/
'up plugin install ./my-plugin' works with relative paths
Validates plugin.json exists and is valid before copying
Auto-registers in registry as enabled
Error if plugin with same name already installed (suggest --force flag)
Shows success message with plugin name and component summary
userStories.notes: Depends on US-003. Simple file copy + registry update. Remote install (git URLs) is a future enhancement.

userStories.title: up init generates plugin directory structure
userStories.description: Update the existing 'up init' command to create the .up/plugins/ directory structure and install builtin plugins.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify src/up/commands/init.py to create .up/plugins/installed/ and .up/plugins/builtin/ directories
Copy builtin plugins (safety, verify) into .up/plugins/builtin/ on init
Create initial registry.json with builtin plugins enabled
Existing init functionality (state.json, config.json, docs/) unchanged
Idempotent: running init again doesn't duplicate plugins
Update init output to show plugin count
userStories.notes: Depends on US-009 and US-010. Final integration step that ties everything together.
